Title: The negative regulator SMAX1 controls mycorrhizal symbiosis and strigolactone biosynthesis in rice

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AM fungal stimulation and hormone signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: D14L is required upstream of SMAX1; d14l mutants show severe AM defects and reduced D27/CYP711A2 expression, linking D14L signaling to SL biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout alleles; AM colonization assay; double-mutant epistasis with smax1; transient localization; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root signaling context; mutant analysis only
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AM inoculation; non-inoculated roots for gene expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D14L is essential for AM colonization. 2. SMAX1 loss suppresses d14l AM defects. 3. D14L positively affects SL biosynthesis by enabling SMAX1 removal.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation of D14L signaling could coordinate AM competence and SL output
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes severe AM deficiency and altered development
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Receptor-like hydrolase for karrikin-related signaling; core experimental gene but not a pathway enzyme or TF

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box/LRR protein; MAX2 ortholog, E3 ubiquitin ligase component
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AM fungal stimulation and hormone signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: D3 acts upstream of SMAX1; d3 mutants are defective in AM colonization, while d3/smax1 restores colonization and shows elevated SL accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; double-mutant epistasis with smax1; transient localization; RNA-seq; LC-MS
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root signaling context; mutant analysis only
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AM inoculation; non-inoculated roots for transcriptomics
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D3 is required for AM symbiosis. 2. SMAX1 acts genetically downstream of D3. 3. d3/smax1 shows strong activation of SL pathway and increased 4-DO.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful signaling node but direct loss impairs AM and development; more practical to modulate downstream repressors
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core experimental gene in signaling, but not classified here as regulation gene because the paper’s focal metabolism regulator is SMAX1 and D3 is treated as upstream signaling component rather than direct transcriptional regulator of metabolic genes

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms committed precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling to arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi; plant hormone function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: D27 transcript is upregulated in smax1 and d3/smax1 and decreased in d14l, consistent with increased strigolactone biosynthesis when SMAX1 is removed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR validation; LC-MS measurement of strigolactones; comparison with d27 mutant as negative control for 4-deoxyorobanchol detection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; induced in smax1 and d3/smax1, decreased in d14l
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Non-inoculated roots for transcriptomics; AM symbiosis context; phosphate-limited growth
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as first committed step of SL biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D27 expression increased upon SMAX1 loss. 2. D27 decreased in d14l. 3. D27 upregulation is part of whole-pathway induction associated with elevated SL accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to elevate root strigolactone output for improved AM recruitment, but caution due to pleiotropic SL effects
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher strigolactones may alter plant architecture or stimulate parasitic weed germination; not directly tested here
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core metabolism gene because the paper explicitly links its transcriptional activation to increased SL production, although direct genetic manipulation of D27 is not the main experimental focus in this study

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves cis-carotenoid precursor in the pathway to carlactone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AM fungal stimulation and hormone signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CCD7 transcripts were elevated in smax1 and d3/smax1, supporting increased SL biosynthetic flux after SMAX1 removal
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; upregulated in smax1 and d3/smax1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCD7 is transcriptionally induced when SMAX1 is absent. 2. Its induction accompanies whole-pathway SL gene activation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful in pathway engineering to boost SL biosynthesis together with upstream regulators
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is transcriptional only in this paper

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of downstream SL products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AM fungal stimulation and hormone signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CCD8 transcripts increased in smax1 and d3/smax1, consistent with transcriptional activation of the full SL pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; upregulated in smax1 and d3/smax1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCD8 is induced after SMAX1 loss. 2. This supports enhanced carlactone formation and SL accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for increasing SL biosynthesis in combination with regulatory edits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is indirect via transcriptional upregulation rather than gene-specific genetics

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: MORE AXILLARY GROWTH 1 / cytochrome P450 CYP711A2
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Carlactonoic acid and subsequently deoxyorobanchol-derived products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation of carlactone to carlactonoic acid followed by reactions contributing to deoxyorobanchol/orobanchol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly contributes to final SL product formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling to AM fungi; developmental hormone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CYP711A2 transcript is upregulated in smax1, d3 and d3/smax1 and reduced in d14l; this aligns with increased SL accumulation when SMAX1 is removed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR validation; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; induced in smax1/d3/smax1, reduced in d14l
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Non-inoculated roots; AM-related and phosphate-limited conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP711A2 is a downstream SL biosynthetic gene activated upon SMAX1 loss. 2. Reduced expression in d14l supports dependence on D14L signaling. 3. Upregulation correlates with increased 4-DO and MeO-5-DS-is1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for modifying canonical rice SL composition and AM signaling output
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could affect shoot branching and rhizosphere interactions; not directly tested here
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper specifically highlights CYP711A2 among five rice MAX1 paralogs as relevant to conversion toward deoxyorobanchol

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SMXL family protein with N-terminal nuclear localization signal and double Clp-N domains
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Strigolactone biosynthesis and AM symbiosis transcriptional program
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes rhizosphere communication with AM fungi; plant hormone roles
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SMAX1 negatively regulates strigolactone biosynthesis and AM symbiosis; loss of SMAX1 derepresses SL biosynthetic genes and increases SL production
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator acting downstream of D14L/D3; its removal is sufficient to restore d14l and d3 AM defects and elevate SL biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional derepression via removal of a suppressor; likely protein degradation downstream of D14L/D3 signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: D27; CCD7; CCD8; CYP711A2/MAX1; NSP2; PT11; AM1; AM3; AM14; NFR5; LYK1; KIN6; SYMRK; CCaMK; CYCLOPS; DLK2a
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses expression of SL biosynthetic genes and multiple AM-associated signaling genes; loss of SMAX1 activates them
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts downstream of D14L/D3 receptor complex; likely targeted for degradation in this signaling module
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: D14L signaling; likely endogenous D14L ligand/karrikin-like signal; AM pre-symbiotic signaling
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback suggested because SMAX1 transcripts are reduced in d14l and d3 while protein stability also appears regulated
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; transcript detectable, protein observed in d14l and d3 protoplasts but not wild type
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Actin1 constitutive promoter for complementation and localization constructs
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AM inoculation with Rhizophagus irregularis; non-inoculated roots for transcriptomics; low phosphate growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; complementation with pActin1::SMAX1; double-mutant epistasis with d14l and d3; transient localization; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; LC-MS strigolactone profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of SMAX1 increases AM fungal colonization. 2. smax1 suppresses the d14l and d3 colonization defects, placing SMAX1 downstream of D14L/D3. 3. SMAX1 removal transcriptionally activates AM symbiosis genes and SL biosynthetic genes. 4. Strigolactone levels increase in the absence of SMAX1.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing/downregulating SMAX1 could enhance AM symbiosis and SL-mediated rhizosphere signaling, but deployment should consider pleiotropic developmental effects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shorter mesocotyls and broad transcriptome changes indicate developmental pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central core gene of the paper; rice homolog of Arabidopsis SMAX1

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AM fungal stimulation and hormone signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NSP2 is highly induced in smax1 and d3/smax1, mirroring the increase in SL biosynthetic gene transcripts
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator of SL biosynthesis noted by the authors
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes induction of SL biosynthetic pathway under certain conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; highly induced in smax1 and d3/smax1
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Non-inoculated roots; low phosphate/AM signaling context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NSP2 transcript abundance rises strongly when SMAX1 is absent. 2. Its induction parallels activation of the full SL biosynthetic pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to boost SL biosynthesis, especially in regulatory engineering stacks
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not genetically manipulated here, but highlighted by authors as regulator of SL production and part of the SMAX1 regulon
